summary: The AFI scoring/DAG plane is implemented by afi-reactor (orchestration) over afi-core (deterministic scoring primitives) and afi-plugins (stub extension templates). There are at least four distinct, partly divergent pipeline/DAG definitions: (1) the runtime Froggy pipeline FROGGY_TREND_PULLBACK_PIPELINE (6 stages) in afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts executed by the linear runPipeline (and a parallel-batch runPipelineDag) in afi-reactor/src/services/pipelineRunner.ts; (2) the declarative codex DAG in afi-reactor/config/dag.codex.json (15 nodes incl. dao-mint-checkpoint/alpha-scout-ingest/execution-sim/froggy-ensemble-scorer) which still lists nodes the runtime pipeline explicitly marks REMOVED; (3) the generic AnalystConfig-driven DAG built programmatically by afi-reactor/src/dag/DAGBuilder.ts + DAGExecutor.ts; and (4) the normative afi-config blueprint.schema.json (id/nodes/edges 'Construct Blueprint') which is a separate graph schema not used by reactor. The actual scoring formulas live in afi-core (canonical UWR computeUwrScore + Froggy analyst scoreFroggyTrendPullbackFromEnriched + NoveltyScorer + SignalDecay), NOT in afi-reactor (which only orchestrates) and NOT in afi-plugins (whose scorer/validator templates are STUB-only, composite=0.0). Replay-critical nodes are the deterministic transforms (telemetry-derive, OHLCV technical+pattern enrichment, the afi-core UWR analyst, novelty) while sentiment/news/AI-ML enrichment, execution-sim, observer/telemetry and the vault-echo are operational-only (external-API or side-effecting, non-deterministic). Multiple authoritative docs across afi-reactor, afi-core and the afi-config governance charter assert afi-reactor is THE 'ONLY'/'canonical' orchestrator and that 'the DAG is law', which directly contradicts the North Star's pluggable Scoring-DAG plane (any conforming orchestrator allowed); these are classified reference-impl-as-law (P1/P2 reactor-only).

answers.question: E1: Map all pipeline/DAG definitions across the scoring plane (froggy, codex, blueprints, plugins) - where is each defined?
answers.answer: There are four+ distinct DAG/pipeline definition surfaces, and they are not a single source of truth. (1) RUNTIME FROGGY PIPELINE: FROGGY_TREND_PULLBACK_PIPELINE is a 6-stage array (uss-telemetry-deriver -> froggy-enrichment-tech-pattern + froggy-enrichment-sentiment-news [parallel] -> froggy-enrichment-adapter -> froggy-analyst -> tssd-vault-write) defined in afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:96-151, declared 'the canonical stage order used by src/services/froggyDemoService.ts' (afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:73). It is executed by runPipeline (linear, afi-reactor/src/services/pipelineRunner.ts:161) and a DAG-aware runPipelineDag that runs levels 'in parallel batches using Promise.all' (afi-reactor/src/services/pipelineRunner.ts:315,339,448). (2) CODEX DAG: a declarative 15-node graph in afi-reactor/config/dag.codex.json (nodes dao-mint-checkpoint:3, tssd-vault-persist:15, full-cognition-loop:24, afi-technical-indicators:40, afi-pattern-detector:52, afi-twitter-sentiment:64, afi-news-reactor:76, afi-ml-ensemble:88, alpha-scout-ingest:100, pixelrick-structurer:113, froggy-enrichment-adapter:126, froggy-analyst-node:139, froggy-ensemble-scorer:156, execution-sim-node:173, froggy-vault-echo:191), advertised as 'canonical orchestrator config' (afi-reactor/README.md:122). (3) GENERIC ANALYST DAG: built programmatically from an AnalystConfig (loaded from afi-factory) by afi-reactor/src/dag/DAGBuilder.ts (buildFromConfig/topologicalSort via Kahn's algorithm) and executed by afi-reactor/src/dag/DAGExecutor.ts. (4) BLUEPRINTS: a normative graph schema 'AFI Construct Blueprint Schema' requiring id/nodes/edges in afi-config/schemas/blueprint.schema.json:3-9 - this is a separate Construct graph contract and is NOT referenced anywhere in afi-reactor (grep 'blueprint' in afi-reactor = 0 hits). PLUGINS: the node implementations are TS plugin modules in afi-reactor/plugins/*.plugin.ts (e.g., afi-reactor/plugins/froggy.trend_pullback_v1.plugin.ts:14) plus the stub template/registry surface in afi-plugins (afi-plugins/src/registry/pluginRegistry.ts). 'froggy' is therefore the live demo pipeline; 'codex' is the declarative/aspirational config; 'blueprints' is an unrelated normative Construct schema; 'plugins' are the node bodies.

answers.question: E2: Which DAG nodes are replay-critical (must reproduce identically on replay) vs operational-only (side-effecting / non-deterministic)?
answers.answer: REPLAY-CRITICAL (deterministic transforms whose outputs must reproduce, per the replay determinism invariant 'Same codex version + same config + same data snapshot -> same validator outputs', afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:37): the OHLCV technical+pattern enrichment ('Technical Indicators - Deterministic Computation ... All functions are deterministic', afi-reactor/src/enrichment/technicalIndicators.ts:2-5); the Froggy analyst node, which is the actual scorer and computes the canonical UWR score deterministically (afi-reactor/plugins/froggy.trend_pullback_v1.plugin.ts:34 calling afi-core computeUwrScore at afi-core/validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts:78-106); and novelty, which is explicitly modelled as replay-stable ('CanonicalNovelty contains ONLY replay-stable fields (no timestamps)', afi-reactor/src/novelty/canonicalNovelty.ts:9-12). The CPJ/USS ingest hash (deterministic SHA256 canonicalization) is also replay-critical. OPERATIONAL-ONLY (external-data, side-effecting, or non-reproducible): the sentiment+news enrichment branch which calls live external APIs (Coinalyze, NewsData.io) (afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:120-126); the AI/ML enrichment-adapter step, which is 'Tiny Brains optional, fail-soft' (afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:128-134); the execution-sim node (dev-only trade simulation, afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:90-94); the observer/telemetry and vault-write/echo persistence sinks (afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:145-150; afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:90-94). CRITICAL CAVEAT: the orchestration layer itself is NOT replay-safe - DAGExecutor generates execution IDs with Date.now()+Math.random() (afi-reactor/src/dag/DAGExecutor.ts:1002-1003) and runs same-level nodes via Promise.all (afi-reactor/src/services/pipelineRunner.ts:448), so even the 'replay-critical' nodes are not bound to a pinned topology/version on the stored record (see Theme A/D). The spec defers UWR/PoI math to afi-core and treats replay as an orchestrator concern (afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:60-62).

answers.question: E3: Where are the analyst / validator / scoring formulas defined (afi-core vs afi-plugins vs afi-reactor)?
answers.answer: The real scoring math lives in afi-core ONLY; afi-reactor orchestrates it and afi-plugins ships stubs. afi-core is the canonical source: (a) the Universal Weighting Rule scorer computeUwrScore - 'UWR = (structure*w_s + execution*w_e + risk*w_r + insight*w_i) / total_weight ... This is the canonical UWR implementation' (afi-core/validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts:62-106), with axes/config types and a default config explicitly flagged as an UNPINNED placeholder ('uwr-default-stub', all weights 0.25, 'TODO: Replace with governance-approved weights', afi-core/validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts:42-52); (b) the per-strategy analyst formula scoreFroggyTrendPullbackFromEnriched + structure/execution/risk/insight axis scorers in afi-core/analysts/froggy.trend_pullback_v1.ts:55-60 feeding the canonical AnalystScoreTemplate (afi-core/src/analyst/AnalystScoreTemplate.ts); (c) the deterministic novelty scorer (afi-core/validators/NoveltyScorer.ts) and time/Greeks decay (afi-core/validators/SignalDecay.ts:27, half-life 24h hardcoded TODO governance); (d) the validator decision envelope with the explicit invariant 'Reputation, PoI, and PoInsight MUST NOT override UWR output or TSSD vault finality' (afi-core/validators/ValidatorDecision.ts:5). afi-reactor does NOT implement scoring math: its froggy-analyst plugin merely calls the afi-core function (afi-reactor/plugins/froggy.trend_pullback_v1.plugin.ts:14,34) and the replay spec confirms 'afi-reactor does not implement UWR math or PoI/PoInsight logic, it only orchestrates' (afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:62). The reactor codex DAG references an 'afi-ensemble-scorer' node with config poiWeight 0.6 / insightWeight 0.4 (afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:90-94) but this is a declarative node config, not an implemented formula in the runtime Froggy pipeline (the live pipeline scores via the afi-core UWR analyst, not a separate PoI/insight ensemble). afi-plugins defines NO real formula: its scorer template is a STUB returning composite 0.0 with the formula only described in a comment 'weighted average: alpha*PoI + beta*PoInsight' (afi-plugins/src/templates/scorer/signalScorerTemplate.ts:100-128).

answers.question: E4: Does any doc claim afi-reactor is THE only orchestrator? Quote verbatim and classify by severity.
answers.answer: Yes - this claim is asserted verbatim in multiple repos and is the core contradiction of this theme. In afi-reactor itself: 'afi-reactor is the ONLY orchestrator in AFI Protocol. All canonical pipelines, DAGs, and routing logic live here.' (afi-reactor/README.md:137); 'This is the ONLY orchestrator in the AFI ecosystem-agents are nodes, not orchestrators.' (afi-reactor/AGENTS.md:3); the Orchestrator Doctrine commandments '### 1. afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI' and '### 3. The DAG is law / Every signal path (ingest -> enrich -> score -> mint/review) must be expressible as a Reactor DAG; ad-hoc flows are anti-patterns.' (afi-reactor/docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:11,38-40); plus source comments 'Reactor-only orchestrator' (afi-reactor/core/dag-engine.ts:2) and the codex 'AFI-Reactor is the canonical orchestrator' (afi-reactor/codex/.afi-codex.json:8). The claim is ECHOED as binding by downstream/governance repos: afi-core 'AFI-Reactor (already hardened) is the canonical orchestrator for AFI Protocol.' (afi-core/docs/AFI_CORE_RUNTIME_OVERVIEW.md:39) and 'afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI - afi-core is NOT an orchestrator.' (afi-core/.factory/droids/schema-validator-droid.md:91), and the globally-binding governance charter names it 'the orchestrator' (afi-config/codex/governance/droids/AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md:117). CLASSIFICATION: this directly contradicts the North Star, which treats the Scoring DAG plane as pluggable - 'afi-reactor, custom DAG, any conforming orchestrator (if outputs conform)' (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:53) and 'AFI must not mandate Mongo or afi-reactor-only conforming artifacts and behavior' (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:71), and explicitly flags this doctrine to 'Reframe: reactor is reference orchestrator' (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:133). Severity: the in-repo self-declaration (README/AGENTS/Doctrine) is P1 (a doc/code framing that would drive external validators to wrong architectural decisions - that a conformant DAG on another stack is forbidden); the downstream echoes in afi-core and the afi-config charter are P2 (reference-impl-as-law, fixable by documentation/scoping).

answers.question: E5: Is the DAG/scoring orchestration itself deterministic/replayable as the doctrine claims?
answers.answer: No - per-node transforms are deterministic but the orchestration layer is not, so the doctrine's claim that DAG orchestration 'ensures ... deterministic execution' is not backed by the executor. The Orchestrator Doctrine asserts 'DAG-based orchestration ensures auditability, reproducibility, and deterministic execution' (afi-reactor/docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:40-42) and the replay spec makes determinism a normative invariant (afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:37). But DAGExecutor.generateExecutionId uses Date.now()+Math.random() (afi-reactor/src/dag/DAGExecutor.ts:1002-1003), same-level nodes are run via Promise.all in parallel batches (afi-reactor/src/services/pipelineRunner.ts:448; afi-reactor/src/dag/DAGExecutor.ts) with no canonical post-merge ordering guarantee, and no pinned topology/version hash is written to the stored signal record (the canonical VaultedSignalRecord pins no dag/plugin/scorer/codex version - see Theme A/D, afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:331-362). The positive offset is that the genuinely score-bearing transforms are individually deterministic (UWR computeUwrScore afi-core/validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts:78-106; technicalIndicators afi-reactor/src/enrichment/technicalIndicators.ts:2-5; replay-stable novelty afi-reactor/src/novelty/canonicalNovelty.ts:9-12), and a no-rescore replay runner stub exists in afi-infra. Net: deterministic re-execution of the scoring DAG is design-only; the orchestration plane lacks the pinning/ordering needed to guarantee it.

findings.title: Two divergent DAG definitions persist: post-purge codex (8-node Scarlet chain) does not match runtime Froggy 6-stage pipeline
findings.evidence: afi-reactor/config/dag.codex.json:1-127 lists 8 nodes (dao-mint-checkpoint through afi-ml-ensemble; legacy alpha-scout/structurer/execution-sim purged 2026-06) while runtime FROGGY_TREND_PULLBACK_PIPELINE is a separate 6-stage graph in afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:96-151; README.md:122 still advertises dag.codex.json as 'canonical orchestrator config'
findings.recommendation: Label dag.codex.json as reference/roadmap (Scarlet enrichment chain) or regenerate it from FROGGY_TREND_PULLBACK_PIPELINE. Post-legacy-purge the dead-node listing is fixed; remaining risk is implementers treating the unrelated 8-node codex as the live Froggy spine.

findings.title: afi-reactor self-declares as THE 'ONLY' orchestrator and 'the DAG is law', presenting the reference scoring-DAG plane as protocol law
findings.evidence: afi-reactor/README.md:137 'afi-reactor is the ONLY orchestrator in AFI Protocol.'; afi-reactor/AGENTS.md:3 'This is the ONLY orchestrator in the AFI ecosystem'; afi-reactor/docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:38-40 'The DAG is law / Every signal path ... must be expressible as a Reactor DAG; ad-hoc flows are anti-patterns' vs North Star pluggable plane afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:53 'afi-reactor, custom DAG, any conforming orchestrator (if outputs conform)'
findings.recommendation: Reframe the doctrine/README per AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:133: afi-reactor is the REFERENCE orchestrator; protocol law is conforming DAG outputs (USS/scored-signal/vaulted-signal) + pinned versions, not that this DAG engine is the only legal orchestrator. Carry into AFI_CONTRADICTION_REGISTER.md (reactor-only) and AFI_REPLAY_READINESS_MATRIX.md.

findings.title: Reactor-as-only-orchestrator framing echoed as binding in afi-core docs and the global governance charter
findings.evidence: afi-core/docs/AFI_CORE_RUNTIME_OVERVIEW.md:39 'AFI-Reactor (already hardened) is the canonical orchestrator for AFI Protocol.'; afi-core/.factory/droids/schema-validator-droid.md:91 'afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI - afi-core is NOT an orchestrator.'; afi-config/codex/governance/droids/AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md:117 '**afi-reactor** is the orchestrator'
findings.recommendation: Scope the charter/runtime-overview 'canonical orchestrator' language to the reference deployment and explicitly state external validators may run any conforming orchestrator that emits USS/TSSD-compliant scored outputs.

findings.title: Canonical UWR scorer ships with an unpinned placeholder config (uwr-default-stub, equal weights), so DAG scores are not reproducible across stacks
findings.evidence: afi-core/validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts:42-52 defaultUwrConfig id 'uwr-default-stub', all weights 0.25, 'TODO: Replace with governance-approved weights sourced from afi-config.'; decay half-life also hardcoded afi-core/validators/SignalDecay.ts:27 'DEFAULT_SIGNAL_HALF_LIFE_HOURS = 24' TODO governance
findings.recommendation: Wire defaultUwrConfig/decay params to versioned afi-config governance configs and record the config id in the scored signal/replay artifact so the same scoring DAG yields identical scores on a third-party stack.

findings.title: Scoring/validation plugin surface (afi-plugins) is STUB-only; the formula a third party would read there is a no-op
findings.evidence: afi-plugins/src/templates/scorer/signalScorerTemplate.ts:100-135 scorer is a STUB returning scoreBreakdown composite 0.0 with the formula only in a comment 'Compute composite score (weighted average: alpha*PoI + beta*PoInsight)'; real scoring is in afi-core (afi-core/validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts:62-106), not the plugin pack
findings.recommendation: Document that afi-plugins is reference scaffolding and that the canonical scoring contract is afi-core UWR/AnalystScoreTemplate; bind plugin scorer/validator manifests to the afi-config plugin-manifest schema so a real scorer can be validated as conformant.

findings.title: DAG orchestration layer is non-deterministic (Math.random execution IDs + parallel Promise.all merges) despite the doctrine's determinism claim
findings.evidence: afi-reactor/src/dag/DAGExecutor.ts:1002-1003 generateExecutionId uses Date.now()+Math.random(); afi-reactor/src/services/pipelineRunner.ts:448 same-level nodes run via Promise.all vs determinism invariant afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:37 and doctrine afi-reactor/docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:40-42
findings.recommendation: Add a pinned topology hash (codex+node/plugin/analyst/validator versions) and an order-independent (or canonically re-sorted) state merge for same-level parallel nodes; record the pinned set per replay session. Shares root cause with Theme A/D record-pinning findings.

findings.title: Per-node scoring transforms are deterministic and replay-stable (positive)
findings.evidence: afi-core/validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts:78-106 pure weighted-average; afi-reactor/src/enrichment/technicalIndicators.ts:2-5 'Deterministic Computation ... All functions are deterministic'; afi-reactor/src/novelty/canonicalNovelty.ts:9-12 'CanonicalNovelty contains ONLY replay-stable fields (no timestamps)'
findings.recommendation: Preserve and promote these canonicalization rules as the determinism artifacts a portable scoring DAG needs; extract them into the protocol spec so other stacks reproduce identical scores/hashes.

contradictions.doc_says: afi-reactor is the ONLY/canonical orchestrator and 'the DAG is law' - every signal path must be expressible as a Reactor DAG.
contradictions.code_does: The North Star defines the Scoring DAG plane as pluggable (afi-reactor / custom DAG / any conforming orchestrator if outputs conform), so a conformant DAG on another stack is explicitly permitted.

contradictions.doc_says: config/dag.codex.json is the 'canonical orchestrator config' / the canonical DAG describing every signal path.
contradictions.code_does: Post-2026-06 legacy purge, dag.codex.json is an 8-node Scarlet enrichment chain (127 lines) that does not match the runtime Froggy 6-stage FROGGY_TREND_PULLBACK_PIPELINE (froggyPipeline.ts:96-151). Legacy demo nodes were removed from codex but the two graphs remain divergent.

contradictions.doc_says: DAG-based orchestration 'ensures auditability, reproducibility, and deterministic execution' and the same codex+config+data yields the same outputs.
contradictions.code_does: The DAG executor generates execution IDs with Math.random and runs same-level nodes via Promise.all with no pinned topology/version on the stored record, so orchestration is not reproducible.
